Title: Factors underlying a latitudinal gradient in the S/G lignin monomer ratio in natural poplar variants
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild-sourced P. trichocarpa association population grown in common garden
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in developing xylem of trees with high S/G lignin ratio
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in developing xylem; higher in genotypes with high S-lignin deposition
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: laccase-mediated polymerization step affecting syringyl, guaiacyl, and catechyl monomer incorporation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for final lignin monomer incorporation and S/G ratio determination
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly polymerizes available monolignols into lignin, biasing flux toward S-unit incorporation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: sinapyl alcohol; coniferyl alcohol; caffeyl alcohol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: monolignol radicals and corresponding S-, G-, and C-linked lignin oligomers
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes monolignols to radicals for lignin polymerization; preferentially oxidizes sinapyl and caffeyl alcohol over coniferyl alcohol and does not oxidize p-coumaryl alcohol under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential expression analysis in extreme S/G genotypes; recombinant protein expression in HEK293 cells; in vitro competition assays; HPLC substrate uptake; LC-MS/MS oligomer detection; structural modeling; site-directed mutagenesis and enzyme kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes higher syringyl-rich lignin composition; biochemical specificity favors S-unit incorporation and increases lignin S/G ratio
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtLAC17-3 transcript abundance is significantly higher in high-S/G poplar xylem. 2. Recombinant PtLAC17-3 preferentially oxidizes sinapyl and caffeyl alcohol over coniferyl alcohol and excludes p-coumaryl alcohol. 3. A single active-site mutation can shift substrate preference toward G-OH, showing that laccase specificity can affect lignin composition.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural latitudinal/climatic variation; warmer lower-latitude origin associated with higher S/G
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; whole-genome resequencing; metabolomics; dataset accession not explicitly provided in paper text, supporting datasets deposited at OSTI
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for engineering lignin composition toward higher S/G for easier delignification and biomass processing; active-site editing may tune monomer preference
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Genetic validation in poplar still required; altered lignin composition may affect wall mechanics, digestibility, water transport, or stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural support, water transport, defense, carbon sequestration, and industrial pulping/biofuel properties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A444T, A444S, and V443F+A444G mutations increased G-OH oxidation relative to wild type; wild type shows about 9-fold higher catalytic efficiency for S-OH and C-OH over G-OH

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild-sourced P. trichocarpa association population grown in common garden
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed as lignin polymerizing gene in developing xylem
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant candidate for natural variation in lignin composition
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: likely affects monolignol incorporation into lignin polymer
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: cell wall / secreted apoplast; paper notes splice variant lacking signal peptide suggesting possible localization differences
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: monolignols (exact preference not experimentally tested here)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: oxidized monolignol radicals for lignin polymerization
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely oxidizes monolignols during lignin polymerization in the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Allele catalog analysis; genotype-phenotype association; geographic allele frequency analysis; sliding-window FST analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: H64D allele is genetically associated with increased lignin S/G ratio and is more frequent at lower latitudes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtLAC4-2 H64D mutation is associated with increased S/G ratio. 2. The H64D allele is enriched in poplars from lower latitudes. 3. Elevated local genetic differentiation around PtLAC4-2 supports spatially varying selection linked to S/G variation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Latitude/temperature-associated natural selection across native range
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing; allele catalog analysis; RNA-seq context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Natural allele marker for selecting high-S/G wood; potential editing target for modifying lignin composition in breeding programs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Functional mechanism unresolved; mutation is outside active site and may affect localization or protein-protein interactions; broader effects on wood properties possible
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural support, water transport, defense, carbon sequestration, and industrial pulping/biofuel properties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PtLAC4-2 was the only lignin gene with a mutation genetically associated with higher S/G ratio showing a geographic pattern; fewer N-glycosylation sites than PtLAC17-3 and a splice variant lacking the signal peptide

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild-sourced P. trichocarpa association population grown in common garden
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in developing xylem of high-S/G trees
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in developing xylem; increased in high S-lignin genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: mid-to-late branch step toward S monolignol formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 5-hydroxylation of guaiacyl-type monolignol precursors to enable syringyl monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript comparison between extreme high- and low-S/G trees
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with increased S-lignin deposition and higher S/G ratio
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtF5H1 is significantly upregulated in high-S/G xylem. 2. The study reiterates F5H as required for S-lignin biosynthesis in poplar. 3. Elevated F5H expression is part of the transcriptional program associated with southern, high-S/G trees.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase S-lignin for improved pulping and biomass deconstruction, potentially in combination with laccase engineering
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: As above for lignin; higher S/G improves delignification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted in the paper’s final model as one of the key biosynthetic genes favoring S-monolignol formation

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild-sourced P. trichocarpa association population grown in common garden
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in developing xylem of high-S/G trees
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in developing xylem; increased in high S-lignin genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: mid-to-late branch step toward S monolignol formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 5-hydroxylation of guaiacyl-type monolignol precursors to enable syringyl monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript comparison between extreme high- and low-S/G trees
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with increased S-lignin deposition and higher S/G ratio
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtF5H2 is significantly upregulated in high-S/G xylem. 2. F5H2 accompanies F5H1 in the S-lignin-enriched expression signature. 3. Differential F5H expression supports altered monolignol biosynthetic flux in natural variants.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target to enhance S-lignin in woody biomass
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: As above for lignin; higher S/G improves delignification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in final pathway model as upregulated in high-S/G trees

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild-sourced P. trichocarpa association population grown in common garden
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Monolignol biosynthetic gene analyzed in natural population
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: entry activation step to hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; natural alleles associated with S/G variation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamates to CoA thioesters that feed lignin and other phenylpropanoid branches
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Two allelic variants (C54Y and E137D) are associated with higher S/G ratio
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pt4CL1 was the only monolignol biosynthetic gene with allelic variants significantly associated with S/G ratio. 2. C54Y and E137D mutations each led to higher S/G ratios. 3. These variants did not show a clear geographic pattern.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural latitudinal variation context, though alleles lacked clear geographic distribution
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential allele-based marker or editing target to tune precursor supply and lignin composition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutations mapped to the AMP-dependent synthetase/ligase domain
